page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture, utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor of the ARMv9 family running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration provides a 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to physical cores with no hyperthreading. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong memory performance, with uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 412.91 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 198.33 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. The Passmark memory score and database operations tests also register in the top tier, with database operations achieving 47617.22. Multi-core performance is notable, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 21484.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 357.08 GB/sec. Single-core operations, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core tests, perform in the average range.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, making it highly suitable for network-intensive applications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 Graviton architecture delivers high resource density and memory bandwidth relative to its resource footprint, though the lack of local storage requires reliance on external EBS volumes. This server is well-suited for workloads such as database operations, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and software compilation, while being less optimal for workloads requiring local NVMe storage or heavy GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture, powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of memory and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached read speeds of 412.91 GB/sec, and strong multi-core performance, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 21484.0. Database operations and multi-threaded decompression also rank in the top tier and strong categories, respectively. This instance is highly suited for database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and network-heavy workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated ARM64 cores.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB of RAM, 100 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and 100 Gbps baseline network performance.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput workloads
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark performance
Best suited for database hosting, software compilation, and web serving
